phase1.explanation:
CSV (Comma-Separated Values) is a common data format:

Writing CSV:
• Use BufferedWriter + FileWriter
• Write header line first
• Each row = field values separated by commas
• Use newLine() for each row

Reading CSV:
• Use BufferedReader + FileReader
• Read header (or skip it)
• Split each line by comma: line.split(",")
• Parse each field (int, double, etc.)
• Create objects from parsed data

Handling edge cases:
• Fields with commas → use quotes: "Alice, Smith"
• Fields with quotes → use escape: "\"text\""
• Empty fields → handle null or empty string
• Different delimiters → semicolon, tab, etc.

Common issues:
• Different line endings (\n, \r\n)
• BOM (Byte Order Mark) at start of file
• Trailing commas

Better approaches:
• Use a CSV library (OpenCSV, Apache Commons CSV)
• Use Scanner with custom delimiter

Data processing:
• Calculate averages
• Filter rows (by year, etc.)
• Sort data
• Transform data

CSV is widely used for:
• Exporting data from databases
• Data exchange between systems
• Excel/Spreadsheet import/export

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates a CSV file called "employees.csv" with header: ID,Name,Department,Salary
2. Writes 4 employee records to the file
3. Reads the file back
4. Calculates and prints the average salary
5. Prints all employees in the "IT" department

Use BufferedReader/BufferedWriter for file I/O.
dialogueHints: Read: split(","), parse values, track sum and count for average
